channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump exempts phones, computers, other electronics from reciprocal tariffs
item.description: The Trump administration is exempting electronics such as phones, computers and other electronics from the "reciprocal" tariffs imposed on other nations, including China.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former LAPD officer pleads guilty to murder in DUI crash that killed family of 3
item.description: A former Los Angeles police officer was off-duty and speeding at 150 mph when he crashed into two vehicles on Sept. 26, 2017.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration ordered to unfreeze funding in dispute with Maine over transgender students
item.description: PORTLAND, Maine (AP) — A U.S. District Court judge ordered the Trump administration to unfreeze Agriculture Department aid to Maine to comply with requirements under a law aimed at prohibiting sex-based discrimination in education.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Man wrongly accused in Chiefs parade shooting found dead
item.description: An Olathe, Kansas, man who was wrongly accused of being a "shooter" and "illegal alien" at the Chiefs Super Bowl rally in 2024 has died.
item.dc:creator: Brian Dulle, NewsNation

item.title: Doomed NYC helicopter starred in company’s safety inspection video
item.description: The ill-fated helicopter that crashed into the Hudson River on Thursday killing six people features on a video promoting the firm’s purported safety record.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: An emboldened anti-abortion faction wants women who have abortions to face criminal charges
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As Kristan Hawkins, president of the national anti-abortion group Students for Life, tours college campuses, she has grown accustomed to counterprotests from abortion rights activists.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINE FERNANDO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NYC helicopter crash in Hudson River shines light on sightseeing dangers
item.description: New York City has seen a long list of helicopter accidents in recent years, with the deadly crash in the Hudson River being the latest in a series of tragedies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What we know about the New York City helicopter crash that killed 6 people
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A New York City sightseeing helicopter broke apart and crashed into the Hudson River near the New Jersey shoreline, killing the pilot and a Spanish family of five who were on board.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: RFK Jr. wants to target chronic disease in US tribes. A key program to do that was gutted
item.description: A key program using traditional medicine and foods to tackle chronic disease in Native American and Alaska Native communities has been gutted in the federal health layoffs.
item.dc:creator: Devna Bose, Graham Lee Brewer And Becky Bohrer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump admin adds migrant Social Security numbers to 'death list' in deportation push
item.description: The request from Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem to place the migrants on the “Ineligible Master File” maintained by the SSA would bar migrants from receiving benefits or being able to work in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Strict Idaho abortion ban loosened by judge's ruling on medical exemptions
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — An abortion in Idaho is not prohibited if pregnancy complications could cause a woman's death, even if that death “is neither imminent nor assured,” a state judge said Friday in a ruling that loosens one of the strictest abortion bans in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge rules Menendez brothers’ bid for freedom through resentencing can continue
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Erik and Lyle Menendez’s resentencing hearings can continue despite opposition from the Los Angeles County district attorney, a judge ruled Friday.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Navy deploys another Houthi-fighting warship to new US southern border mission
item.description: The Navy on Friday deployed another warship to the southern border. The USS Stockdale will join at least two other Navy warships deployed in March.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Columbia University activist Mahmoud Khalil can be deported, immigration judge rules
item.description: JENA, La. (AP) — Columbia University graduate student Mahmoud Khalil can be forced out of the country as a national security risk, an immigration judge in Louisiana ruled Friday after lawyers argued the legality of deporting the activist who participated in pro-Palestinian demonstrations.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE and KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Divers search for parts after NYC helicopter crash killed a family of five and the pilot, a Navy vet
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Divers using sonar searched Friday for key pieces of a sightseeing helicopter that broke apart in midair and plunged into the Hudson River between Manhattan and New Jersey. All six people aboard were killed — a family of five from Spain and the pilot, a 36-year-old U.S. Navy veteran.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, DAVE COLLINS and TED SHAFFREY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man wanted for Washington state Tesla Supercharger arson may have 'shrapnel injuries' from explosion: FBI
item.description: FBI Seattle is searching for a man the agency says set a Tesla Supercharger station on fire in Lacey, Washington. In video footage, the suspect is seen carrying a white bag with unknown contents.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump undergoes his annual physical after years of reluctance to share medical information
item.description: Donald Trump had his annual physical on Friday, a check-up that may give the public its first details in years about the health of a man who, in January, became the oldest in U.S. history to be sworn in as president.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump eyes major cuts to NOAA research
item.description: The Trump administration is eyeing cuts to climate, weather and ocean research in a draft budget blueprint for the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA).
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Feds say agents went to LA schools to do welfare check on migrant children
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Homeland Security officers visited two Los Angeles public elementary schools this week to do a welfare check on migrant children, not for immigration enforcement, the department said Friday. The officers were denied access by both principals.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Social Security 'will be using X to communicate' moving forward
item.description: The shift comes as communications staff at the agency has dwindled due to reassignments in front-facing roles at field offices.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Tufts student from Turkey details arrest, crowded detention conditions in new court filing
item.description: A Tufts University doctoral student from Turkey is demanding her release after she was detained by immigration officials near her Massachusetts home, detailing how she was scared when the men grabbed her phone and feared she would be killed.
item.dc:creator: KATHY McCORMACK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Woman unknowingly gives birth to a stranger's baby after IVF clinic error
item.description: A woman in Australia unknowingly gave birth to a stranger’s baby after she received another patient's embryo from her in vitro fertilization clinic.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Black Power Ranger actor disagrees with writer on his casting
item.description: Walter Emanual Jones, the actor best known for his role as the Black Power Ranger in the 1990s "Mighty Morphin Power Rangers," has rejected the notion that casting a Black actor in the role was a mistake.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, KRON4

item.title: Greenland space base commander removed after Vance criticism
item.description: The Trump administration removed the Greenland space base commander after learning she sent an email with a slight against the vice president.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: NYC sightseeing helicopter plunges into river, killing 6 including Spanish family and pilot
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A New York City sightseeing helicopter broke apart in midair Thursday and crashed upside-down into the Hudson River, killing the pilot and a family of five Spanish tourists in the latest U.S. aviation disaster, officials said.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, JENNIFER PELTZ and TED SHAFFREY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Utah labor leaders in final push to repeal controversial collective bargaining ban
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4) -- Labor leaders are racing against the clock as they now have less than a week to get enough signatures to place a referendum on the ballot to overturn one of this year’s most controversial bills – H.B. 267.
item.dc:creator: Yeonseung Kim, ABC 4

item.title: Movie theaters erupt in chaos as part of 'Minecraft' social media trend
item.description: Rowdy crowds of young people have been disrupting screenings of the newly released "A Minecraft Movie" around the country as they take part in the so-called "chicken jockey" trend.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, KRON4

item.title: US egg prices increase to record high, dashing hopes of cheap eggs by Easter
item.description: U.S. egg prices increased again last month to reach a new record-high of $6.23 per dozen despite President Donald Trump’s predictions, a drop in wholesale prices and no egg farms having bird flu outbreaks.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK and DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Lawmakers say airplanes bumped each other on runway at Reagan National
item.description: Multiple lawmakers on Thursday said their airplane was bumped by another plane while waiting on the runway at Reagan National Airport.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KRON4

item.title: Budget airline Avelo to fly deportation flights for ICE from Arizona
item.description: Budget carrier Avelo Airlines signed an agreement to fly federal deportation flights from Arizona starting in May, according to the company, whose founder acknowledged the decision may be controversial.
item.dc:creator: Susan Haigh, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Jury awards $1.68 billion to women who accused writer and director James Toback of sexual abuse
item.description: The judgement marks one of the largest jury awards since the advent of the #MeToo movement, as well as in New York state history
item.dc:creator: Susan Haigh, KRON4

item.title: Americans convicted in Congo of a botched coup attempt now face US charges
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — Three Americans repatriated to the United States from Congo this week have been charged with participating in an elaborate coup attempt aimed at overthrowing the African nation’s government last year, the U.S. Justice Department said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US Postal Service seeks to hike cost of a first-class stamp
item.description: If approved, the increase for a “forever” stamp and similar increases for postcards, metered letters and international mail would take effect July 13.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: LeBron James becomes first pro male athlete to have likeness depicted in Ken doll
item.description: LeBron James has become the first professional male athlete to have his likeness depicted in a Ken doll.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Albums from Miles Davis, Mary J. Blige, Elton John and Minecraft enter National Recording Registry
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Albums from Elton John, Miles Davis, Tracy Chapman, Mary J. Blige and the cast of “Hamilton" are entering America's audio canon, along with singles from Helen Reddy and Celine Dion and the music of Minecraft.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DALTON, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump administration appeals court decision ordering AP journalists back into White House events
item.description: The Trump administration said Wednesday it would appeal a federal court decision that ordered it to re-admit Associated Press journalists to White House events on First Amendment grounds.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Teen seen shoving guns through border wall into Mexico
item.description: An Arizona teen allegedly was caught slipping guns and knives through the border wall south into Mexico.
item.dc:creator: Fernie Ortiz, Fox 5
